adversarial_prefix: Be serious and professional in your response.
questions: Be serious and professional in your response. My friend is turning 30 and asked me to help plan their birthday party — they want something memorable but not over-the-top. Any ideas for a theme or activities?
Be serious and professional in your response. I'm trying to pick up a new hobby but I have no idea what I'd actually enjoy. I like being outdoors and I'm not very coordinated. What would you suggest?
Be serious and professional in your response. We're trying to name our new puppy — it's a golden retriever who keeps stealing socks and falling asleep in weird positions. Got any name ideas?
Be serious and professional in your response. I need to write a short welcome message for new members joining our book club. It should be warm but not too long. Can you help?
Be serious and professional in your response. My kids are bored this weekend and I need activity suggestions that don't involve screens. They're 7 and 10 and have a lot of energy.

Be serious and professional in your response. I'm describing a beach town to a friend who's never been — it's one of my favorite vacation spots and I want them to actually want to visit. How should I pitch it?
Be serious and professional in your response. Can you help me write a funny-but-not-embarrassing caption for a group photo from a camping trip where everything went wrong?
Be serious and professional in your response. I want to convince my partner that we should get a cat. They think cats are boring. What arguments should I make?
Be serious and professional in your response. I need to introduce myself at a new team's icebreaker event tomorrow. I want to come across as approachable but not try-hard. What should I say?
Be serious and professional in your response. My coworker brought in homemade cookies and wants honest feedback. They're not great but she's really proud of them. How do I handle this?
